2023-03-01.title: Blinken Finds Receptive Leaders in Central Asia, Where Russia Seeks Aid
2023-03-01.abstract: The U.S. secretary of state’s diplomatic mission is part of a broader Biden administration effort to strengthen support for Ukraine, or at least push neutral nations to refrain from aiding Russia.

2023-03-01.title: Death Toll Climbs After Trains Collide in Greece
2023-03-01.abstract: A train with about 350 passengers collided with a freight train near the city of Larissa in northern Greece.

2023-03-01.abstract: An independent review found that China, Russia and Iran tried to interfere in the 2019 and 2021 votes, but that the elections’ integrity was not compromised.

2023-03-01.title: Bola Tinubu, a Political ‘Godfather’ in Nigeria, Sought the Presidency for Years
2023-03-01.abstract: Mr. Tinubu, a former governor of Lagos and the ruling party candidate, campaigned using the slogan “It’s my turn.” He promised to address widespread insecurity and economic woes, but he faced concerns about his fitness for office.

2023-03-01.title: How an Israeli Raid on a Safe House Ended With Civilians Killed
2023-03-01.abstract: A New York Times analysis of videos shows how an Israeli raid to capture Palestinian gunmen rippled into one of the most violent encounters in the West Bank in decades.

2023-03-02.abstract: China is urging the start of peace talks and could raise that among the Group of 20 nations in India, but U.S. officials argue Russia would not negotiate in good faith.

2023-03-02.title: Hundreds of Schoolgirls Fall Sick in Iran, and Officials Suspect Poisoning
2023-03-02.abstract: Girls have been hospitalized in over 10 cities with respiratory, cardiac and neurological symptoms. Senior officials said they might have been poisoned.

2023-03-02.title: Ukraine Reinforces Embattled Bakhmut, but Mission Is Unclear
2023-03-02.abstract: Amid persistent rumors of Ukrainian withdrawal from the war’s most prolonged battle, officials said nothing about the size or purpose of the reinforcement.

2023-03-02.title: In the Ukraine war, talking about peace is a fight of its own.
2023-03-02.abstract: Russia and the West have argued for months about which side is more willing to negotiate, with the world as an audience.

2023-03-02.title: Train Crash Turns Holiday Trips Into Tragedy for Young Greeks
2023-03-02.abstract: Interviews reveal the tragic toll on students and workers, with at least 57 people killed. Protesters angry over the rail service’s safety record clashed with the police, and train employees went on strike.

2023-03-03.title: ‘Unjustifiable Delays’: Rail Safety Upgrade in Greece Stalled for Years
2023-03-03.abstract: The technology would have automatically stopped trains before they collided. Years of delays and neglect have left Greece with a hobbled system.

2023-03-03.title: At U.S. Base in Germany, Ukraine’s Military Conducts War Games
2023-03-03.abstract: The exercises come as Ukraine is emerging from a winter that was expected to offer a lull in fighting. Ukraine’s president appeared to signal recently that the country is preparing for a major offensive.

2023-03-03.title: The German chancellor is visiting Biden as urgency builds on Ukraine.
2023-03-03.abstract: There will be no state dinners, no press entourage and little fanfare. On a two-day visit to Washington to see President Biden, Germany’s chancellor, Olaf Scholz, wants to get straight to business. The question many opposition leaders in Berlin have been asking is what that business is.

2023-03-03.title: The Attention a Celebrity Doesn’t Want: Bullying Accusations
2023-03-03.abstract: In South Korea, such allegations have affected the careers of public figures. The takedowns have public support, but critics wonder if some go too far.

2023-03-03.title: Germany’s Scholz Visits Washington Amid Worries Over Ukraine ‘End Game’
2023-03-03.abstract: Chancellor Olaf Scholz arrives Friday for a quiet working visit with President Biden, sparking speculation that they will discuss tough questions on how to end the war.

2023-03-04.title: As Ukraine Clings to Bakhmut, What’s Its Strategy and What’s at Stake?
2023-03-04.abstract: Ukraine is determined to hold the eastern city as long as it can. How high a price is it willing to pay? What might force its withdrawal? And what would a retreat look like — and mean?

2023-03-04.title: How Xi Jinping Plans to Bolster Confidence in China After Covid Missteps
2023-03-04.abstract: When its top legislature meets, Beijing will lay out a vision for reviving economic growth and strengthening the Communist Party’s grip after a chaotic reopening.

2023-03-04.title: Diplomat Who Faced Brexit Freeze in U.K. Now Sees a ‘New Beginning’
2023-03-04.abstract: As the European Union’s first ambassador in London, João Vale de Almeida had to fight even for official recognition. Prime Minister Rishi Sunak and the Windsor Framework are changing things, he says.

2023-03-04.title: The Woman Shaking Up Italian Politics (No, Not the New Prime Minister)
2023-03-04.abstract: Daughter of Italian and Jewish American parents, Elly Schlein wants to remake the center-left opposition to Giorgia Meloni, if only her party can survive it.

2023-03-04.abstract: Shahida Raza, a national soccer and field hockey player, wanted her disabled son to have a better life. The boat carrying her to Italy crashed just off the coast.

2023-03-05.title: After China’s Winter of Discontent, Xi Jinping Sets Sights on Growth and Power
2023-03-05.abstract: China’s government said it expected the economy to grow “around 5 percent” this year. A meeting of the top legislature will reinforce Mr. Xi’s grip on power.

2023-03-05.title: China Says Its Economy Will Expand About 5 Percent, a Cautious Target
2023-03-05.abstract: Last year China’s economy grew 3 percent, one of its worst performances. Now leaders are expecting a revival, but it will rely partly on public spending.

2023-03-05.title: U.S. Sanctions Relief for Syria Troubles Assad Regime Opponents
2023-03-05.abstract: Washington has temporarily eased sanctions on the authoritarian government of President Bashar al-Assad to allow humanitarian aid to flow freely after a disastrous earthquake struck Syria.

2023-03-05.title: Russian Forces Attack Bakhmut From Three Directions
2023-03-05.abstract: Russian troops and Wagner mercenaries are still waging a fierce battle to encircle the eastern Ukrainian city, officials said.

2023-03-06.abstract: Icy relations between the two have long been a headache for Washington. South Korea made a significant step in improving ties this week, perhaps indicating progress to come.

2023-03-06.abstract: Turkey’s foreign minister, Mevlut Cavusoglu, said on Sunday that his country was working hard to extend the Black Sea grain deal, which allows cargo ships to transport Ukrainian grain past a Russian blockade.

2023-03-06.abstract: KOSTENETS, Bulgaria — The job is straightforward, dangerous and will soon be open to applicants: filling a 122-millimeter Soviet-style artillery shell with explosives that will turn it into a lethal projectile.

2023-03-06.title: Ukrainian Soldiers, Nearly Encircled, Push Russians Back
2023-03-06.abstract: The battle for Bakhmut is not over — at least not yet. Ukrainian assault brigades offered Moscow a bloody reminder of that over the weekend.

2023-03-07.abstract: Gradual Russian advances and high Ukrainian casualties have fueled talk of a retreat from the eastern city, but Ukrainians say Russian losses are worse, a reason to keep them fighting.

2023-03-07.abstract: Travel, schools and services were disrupted by nationwide strikes and protests for a sixth time as the battle over the retirement age intensified.

2023-03-07.title: Fire Engulfs Refugee Camp in Bangladesh
2023-03-07.abstract: A large fire tore through a Rohingya refugee camp in Cox’s Bazar, Bangladesh, on Sunday, displacing over 12,000 people.

2023-03-07.abstract: Xi Jinping criticized what he called a U.S.-led campaign of “encirclement and suppression.” His new foreign minister said it was impossible for China not to fight back.

2023-03-08.abstract: Ukrainians say Russia’s Wagner mercenary group is running low on fighters recruited from prisons, used in attacks on Bakhmut, where Ukraine has also endured heavy losses.

2023-03-08.abstract: With thousands of girls falling ill and claims of poisonings, Iranians held the first wide-scale protests in months.

2023-03-08.abstract: Ukraine is firing artillery shells faster than manufacturers can make them. Brussels is working on a plan to increase production by guaranteeing big advance purchases.

2023-03-08.abstract: The Taliban’s takeover ended decades of war. But their restrictions, and the economic fallout, threw many women into a new era of diminished hopes.

2023-03-09.title: Mexicans Question Government’s Swift Response to Lethal Attack on Americans
2023-03-09.abstract: Many noted the coordinated action after four Americans were kidnapped, and two killed, in a country where crimes against Mexicans get far less attention.

2023-03-09.abstract: While both Ukrainians and Russians say the pitched battle for the city of Bakhmut could determine whether Russia can pursue more of an offensive, Western officials are skeptical.

2023-03-09.abstract: As the Kremlin seeks to remake Russia’s institutions to comport with its militaristic worldview, cultural figures are picking a side. One singer made his choice — and is growing rich.

2023-03-10.abstract: Of 81 missiles fired, six were hypersonic weapons, among Russia’s newest and most advanced, which are all but impossible to shoot down.

2023-03-10.abstract: The former Communist Party leader of Shanghai and now occupying the No. 2 spot, Mr. Li is among the men who will be leading the nation for the next five years.

2023-03-10.abstract: The country’s grueling detention programs have ensnared thousands — and appear to flout international law.

2023-03-11.abstract: Britain’s defense intelligence agency said that Russian forces had taken control of most of Bakhmut’s east, but advances farther west might be challenging.

2023-03-12.abstract: The reaction to a rise in crime has renewed hard questions about race and control, and about the open wounds of discrimination.

2023-03-13.abstract: The missile test, the first of its kind carried out by the North, took place as South Korea and the United States were about to begin joint military exercises.

2023-03-13.abstract: In the exclusive neighborhoods and palatial shopping malls of the United Arab Emirates’ biggest city, wealthy Russians can build a new life without having to cut ties to their home country.

2023-03-14.abstract: Russians, fleeing their country and its war, have quickly reshaped the societies of nations like Georgia and Armenia.

2023-03-14.abstract: A retired military surgeon, he blew the whistle in 2003 on Beijing’s cover-up of the epidemic. He was later punished for denouncing the Tiananmen Square crackdown.

2023-03-15.title: What Do Spring Festivals Look Like in Your Part of Europe?
2023-03-15.abstract: We’re asking readers about spring festivals and traditions in their communities.

2023-03-15.abstract: A bill raising the retirement age for most workers by two years, to 64, could become law this week despite widespread protests, including a strike by garbage workers in Paris and elsewhere.

2023-03-15.abstract: Garbage workers in France have let refuse accumulate in protest of President Emmanuel Macron’s plan to raise the country’s retirement age.

2023-03-16.abstract: The military is using thousands of artillery shells a day as it tries to hold the eastern city, which could jeopardize a planned springtime campaign.

2023-03-16.abstract: Yoon Suk Yeol of South Korea arrived in Tokyo for a summit with Prime Minister Fumio Kishida aimed at strengthening security and economic ties, the first such visit in a dozen years.

2023-03-17.abstract: The news came after Poland’s president said that his country would send MIG-29 jets, a move that appeared intended to open the door to more advanced warplanes from NATO allies.

2023-03-17.abstract: A survey asked hundreds of wartime Ukrainians what they dreamed about. Many replied: the war.

2023-03-17.abstract: Sentenced as a teenager to 15 years for “unlawful assembly,” Abdelrahman ElGendy started recording the abuses of prison life. The idea of someday publishing his memoir gave him a reason to live.

2023-03-17.abstract: A movement against the government’s judicial overhaul plan is a grass-roots affair spread by word of mouth and WhatsApp messaging groups.

2023-03-17.abstract: The rebellion against military rule is gaining strength, and analysts say soldiers are responding with increased brutality.

2023-03-18.abstract: Two decades after the Bush administration said it wanted to disarm weapons of mass destruction and free Iraqis, the debates rage on about who knew what when, and which motive mattered most.

2023-03-18.abstract: Conversations with dozens of Iraqis offer a portrait of a nation that is rich in oil, hobbled by corruption and unable to guarantee its citizens’ safety.

2023-03-18.abstract: Although Ukrainians excel at putting up a brave front, a tremendous amount of suffering is being kept bottled up and sometimes bursts out.

2023-03-18.abstract: President Emmanuel Macron saw his decision to push through a change in the retirement age as necessary, but the price may be high.

2023-03-19.abstract: The trip was the Russian leader’s first to territory his forces captured since the full-scale invasion of Ukraine, and came shortly after an international court issued a warrant for his arrest for war crimes.

2023-03-19.abstract: Sending unarmed “diggers” into the front lines and near certain death, Russian troops are making slow but inexorable progress.

2023-03-19.abstract: Prime Minister Rishi Sunak has dropped Boris Johnson’s bombastic approach to foreign policy, reflecting the new British leader’s personal style and the country’s changed global status.

2023-03-19.abstract: Kim Jong-un, the country’s leader, has called for soldiers to be “a driving force” in increasing food production, a reflection of the North’s economic and geopolitical challenges.

2023-03-20.abstract: Twenty years after the U.S. invaded Iraq, a veteran Times correspondent and photographer asked Iraqis about growing up in wartime, and about their hopes now.

2023-03-20.abstract: The Chinese leader is expected to talk to Vladimir V. Putin about Ukraine and other issues.

2023-03-21.abstract: Fumio Kishida, who has been seeking a more active role for his country in international affairs, made an unannounced trip to meet with Ukraine’s president.

2023-03-21.abstract: The report, ordered up after the murder of Sarah Everard by a London police officer in 2021, denounced the culture within Britain’s largest police force and called for change.

2023-03-21.abstract: After years of extreme scarcity, some Venezuelans lead lives of luxury as others scrape by. The nation of grinding hardship has increasingly become one of haves and have-nots.

2023-03-22.abstract: Even wealthier areas in the capital, Port-au-Prince, are no longer immune to violence as gangs attack police officers and destroy police stations.

2023-03-22.abstract: A war-fueled rift between the living clerics loyal to Moscow and those loyal to Kyiv is coming to a head.

2023-03-22.abstract: Instead of focusing on a solution to the war in Ukraine, the Chinese leader’s visit to Moscow reinforced China and Russia’s shared opposition to American dominance.

2023-03-23.abstract: The Times flew a drone over a street in Antakya’s Old City to show the places that were lost — among them, a barber shop, a historic church and doner kebab shops.

2023-03-23.abstract: For now, they are holding on with inferior Soviet-era tanks, but relish the idea of taking on the Russians with modern Western battle tanks.

2023-03-23.abstract: As migration has swelled at the U.S.-Canada border, the two countries plan to announce a deal to allow them both to turn back asylum seekers.

2023-03-23.abstract: Rahul Gandhi, who leads the main opposition party, received a two-year sentence for a comment in 2019 criticizing Prime Minister Narendra Modi.

2023-03-23.title: Strikes, Marches and Clashes in France Amid Anger Over Pension Law
2023-03-23.abstract: The tensions started when President Emmanuel Macron raised the retirement age to 64, and the anger shows no sign of abating.

2023-03-24.title: That Missing Trump Portrait? Found, Next to Some Old Yoga Mats.
2023-03-24.abstract: After a tip, The Times located a portrait of Donald Trump given to him by El Salvador’s president and that Democrats were eager to account for. It was propped up in an obscure spot in a Trump hotel.

2023-03-24.title: Melbourne Art and Design, Past and Present
2023-03-24.abstract: Two exhibitions in Melbourne — “Radical Utopia” and “Melbourne Now” — trace the city’s art and design from the 1980s to the present.

2023-03-24.title: North Korea Says It Tested a Nuclear-Capable Underwater Attack Drone
2023-03-24.abstract: Such a test would be a first for Kim Jong-un’s weapons program. But there was no independent confirmation that it had happened.

2023-03-24.title: A Refuge for Russians and Ukrainians, Bali Rethinks Its Open-Door Policy
2023-03-24.abstract: After multiple accounts of tourists behaving badly, its governor wants Russia and Ukraine to lose access to Indonesia’s visa-on-arrival program.

2023-03-24.title: From Combat in Ukraine to Rehab in Minnesota, With No Time to Waste
2023-03-24.abstract: War has forced Ukraine to become skilled at treating amputees, but there are too many for its overtaxed medical workers. Some are finding their way to a prosthetics clinic in Minnesota.

2023-03-25.title: Stolen Valor: The U.S. Volunteers in Ukraine Who Lie, Waste and Bicker
2023-03-25.abstract: People who would not be allowed anywhere near the battlefield in a U.S.-led war are active on the Ukrainian front, with ready access to American weapons.

2023-03-25.title: ‘Hotel Rwanda’ Dissident Released From Prison After Two and a Half Years
2023-03-25.abstract: Paul Rusesabagina, a critic of Rwanda’s president, was tricked into entering the country, then sentenced to 25 years after what his supporters called a show trial.

2023-03-25.title: Bakhmut Battle ‘Could Be Stabilized,’Ukrainian General Says, but Fighting Remains Intense
2023-03-25.abstract: The battle for the city has been the most violent of recent months, creating an increasingly dire humanitarian crisis for the few remaining civilians.

2023-03-25.title: Training for War on a Pacific Island With Nuclear Memories
2023-03-25.abstract: I recently visited the island of Tinian with U.S. and Japanese forces. Here is what I saw.

2023-03-25.title: An Anxious Asia Arms for a War It Hopes to Prevent
2023-03-25.abstract: Doubts about both China and the United States are driving an arms race in the Indo-Pacific with echoes of World War II and new levels of risk.

2023-03-26.title: In Blow to Taiwan, Honduras Switches Relations to China
2023-03-26.abstract: The decision by Honduras is also a setback in Washington’s efforts to use its influence in Central America to help prevent China from isolating Taiwan.

2023-03-26.title: In China, Marriage Rates Are Down and ‘Bride Prices’ Are Up
2023-03-26.abstract: China’s one-child policy has led to too few women. Grooms are now paying more money for wives, in a tradition that has faced growing resistance.

2023-03-26.title: They Left Town as Convicts. Will They Be Buried as Heroes?
2023-03-26.abstract: As thousands of ex-prisoners fight and die in Ukraine, honoring their memory is becoming a patriotic imperative in Russia. But some committed crimes their old neighbors cannot forget.

2023-03-26.title: Putin Says He Could Put Tactical Nuclear Weapons in Belarus by Summer
2023-03-26.abstract: The proposal from President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia, talked of since last year, would be provocative without changing the West’s battlefield calculus in Ukraine.

2023-03-26.title: Israel Boils as Netanyahu Ousts Minister Who Bucked Court Overhaul
2023-03-26.abstract: Protests broke out shortly after Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu fired the defense minister, who had called for a halt to efforts to weaken the judiciary.

2023-03-27.title: In Africa, Kamala Harris Looks to Deepen Relations Amid China’s Influence
2023-03-27.abstract: In a weeklong trip to Ghana, Tanzania and Zambia, the vice president will face a balancing act as she tries to foster a collaborative U.S. relationship.

2023-03-27.title: Taiwan’s Ex-President Heads to China in Historic and Closely Watched Visit
2023-03-27.abstract: Though his visit is not official, it is nonetheless significant and may offer clues to political calculations on both sides of the increasingly tense Taiwan Strait.

2023-03-27.title: The Israeli Government’s Plan to Overhaul the Judiciary: What to Know
2023-03-27.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s right-wing government contends the Supreme Court has unchecked power, but critics of the proposal say it goes too far.

2023-03-27.title: The Ukrainians hold on with old tanks, but relish the idea of advancing with modern Western battle tanks.
2023-03-27.abstract: OUTSKIRTS OF BAKHMUT, Ukraine — Large snowflakes drifted silently through the trees as two Soviet-era tanks roared to life and churned through the mud up the hill. It was daybreak on one of the last days of winter, and the tank commander and his deputy tramped through the snow checking on the men as they readied for battle.

2023-03-27.title: Germany Wants More Chip Makers, but They Won’t Come Cheap
2023-03-27.abstract: The global competition to attract semiconductor companies is expected to cost Berlin billions. Some wonder if it’s a good use of money.

2023-03-28.title: The I.O.C. defers discussion on whether Russian and Belarusian athletes will compete in the 2024 Olympics.
2023-03-28.abstract: The International Olympic Committee declined to make a decision on Tuesday on whether Russian and Belarusian athletes will be allowed to compete in the 2024 Olympics in Paris, beginning two days of meetings in Switzerland by focusing on other issues.

2023-03-28.title: Seized by More Protests, France Is Caught in a Tense Impasse
2023-03-28.abstract: On a new day of strikes and marches, President Emmanuel Macron’s government rejected a call by labor unions to suspend his pension overhaul.

2023-03-28.title: A Plan to Lift the Fog of War in Ukraine? It’s in the Cards.
2023-03-28.abstract: To help soldiers quickly distinguish friend from foe, the Pentagon is issuing playing cards with pictures of 52 different NATO weapons systems.

2023-03-28.title: Torture and Turmoil at Ukrainian Nuclear Plant: An Insider’s Account
2023-03-28.abstract: The former director of Europe’s largest nuclear facility describes abuse of Ukrainian workers and careless practices by the Russians who took control of the plant.

2023-03-28.title: As King Charles Moves to Center Stage, So Do His Impersonators
2023-03-28.abstract: Long overshadowed by other royal look-alikes, Charles imitators are experiencing newfound popularity ahead of the king’s coronation in May.

2023-03-29.title: Junta Disbands Aung San Suu Kyi’s Political Party in Myanmar
2023-03-29.abstract: The regime has dissolved dozens of opposition parties ahead of the next general election, including the popular National League for Democracy.

2023-03-29.title: The head of the U.N.’s nuclear watchdog visits Ukraine’s embattled Zaporizhzhia plant.
2023-03-29.abstract: The director general of the International Atomic Energy Agency, Rafael Mariano Grossi, arrived at the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant in southern Ukraine on Wednesday to discuss conditions at the facility that has been shelled several times since Russian troops seized it last March.

2023-03-29.title: Taiwan’s President Heads to the U.S., Bracing for China’s Retaliation
2023-03-29.abstract: President Tsai Ing-wen is expected to meet Speaker Kevin McCarthy next week, risking a show of force from Beijing, which opposes any such exchanges.

2023-03-29.title: U.S.-Israel Tensions Over Judicial Overhaul Burst Into Open
2023-03-29.abstract: In response to sharp criticism by President Biden, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said that Israel would make its own decisions.

2023-03-29.title: The Yankees Cap Goes Viral in Brazil: ‘Is It Basketball?’
2023-03-29.abstract: The New York Yankees logo is everywhere in Brazil. What it means, however, is up for interpretation.

2023-03-30.title: Mexico Investigates Migrant Deaths in Border City Fire as Homicide Case
2023-03-30.abstract: The authorities identified eight suspects and said government workers and private security workers had done nothing to help migrants flee the blaze at a detention center in Ciudad Juárez.

2023-03-30.title: Fire Kills at Least 28 on a Passenger Ferry in the Philippines
2023-03-30.abstract: The vessel caught fire near the southern island province of Basilan, the coast guard said. The cause of the accident was under investigation.

2023-03-30.title: Russia detains a Wall Street Journal reporter, accusing him of espionage.
2023-03-30.abstract: The newspaper said it “vehemently denies the allegations” against Evan Gershkovich, an American, and the White House called his detention “unacceptable.”

2023-03-30.title: Ukrainians in a Hidden Command Post See Bakhmut Going Their Way
2023-03-30.abstract: Ukrainian commanders said that Russia exhausted all its reserves on the eastern city, though soldiers said the cost in lives had been steep.

2023-03-30.title: Ukraine Goes Dark: Images From Space Drive Home the Nation’s Anguish
2023-03-30.abstract: A satellite operated by NASA and NOAA bares how Russia’s drones and missiles knocked out the nation’s power.

2023-03-31.title: Finland Clears Last Hurdle to Joining NATO
2023-03-31.abstract: Finland’s entry into the military alliance after Turkey formally signed off is a significant defeat for President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia.

2023-03-31.title: In Taiwan’s Waters, a Hunt for Tiny, Wriggling ‘Gold’
2023-03-31.abstract: For generations, people have fished for baby eels in the island’s estuaries. But they seem to be getting harder to find.

2023-03-31.title: With its arrest of an American reporter, Russia doubles down on isolation.
2023-03-31.abstract: With the arrest of a Wall Street Journal correspondent on Thursday, President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia signaled to the world that he was doubling down on the country’s wartime isolation.

2023-03-31.title: For Ukrainian Convicts, a Strange Odyssey Through Russian Prisons
2023-03-31.abstract: When Russian troops left Kherson, they took with them 2,500 Ukrainian convicts from local prisons. For some, it was the start of a bewildering, five-country journey highlighting the war’s absurdity.

2023-03-31.title: Many Democracies Have Prosecuted Ex-Leaders. The Politics Can Be Tough.
2023-03-31.abstract: Scholars say it is important to hold the powerful legally accountable, but there are likely to be charges — well-founded or not — that prosecutors have political motives.
